issues.blurb: Coaster and hand brakes drift out of adjustment, and a child needs brakes that are easy to reach and reliably stop the bike.
Small tires lose pressure quickly and pick up flats, and underinflated tires make pedaling harder and riding less stable for a child.
Handlebar, seat, and training-wheel bolts work loose from use and rough handling, which can shift parts mid-ride if not checked.
Chains loosen, slip, or rust when bikes are left outside, leading to skipping pedals and a chain that can drop off.
Children outgrow their bikes fast, and a frame or saddle set too small or too large makes the bike awkward and harder to control.

narrative.headline: What kids' bike owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by parents of kids' bikes as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Brake adjustment, tire pressure, and loose bolts tend to come up most.

issues.title: Brake Adjustment
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~24%) — coaster and hand brakes drift out of adjustment and need to be easy for a child to use.

issues.title: Tire Pressure & Flats
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — small tires lose pressure fast and pick up flats, making riding harder and less stable.

issues.title: Loose Bolts (Bars, Seat & Training Wheels)
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — handlebar, seat, and training-wheel bolts work loose and can shift mid-ride.

issues.title: Chain Tension & Rust
issues.blurb: Often reported (~18%) — chains loosen, slip, or rust when left outside, causing skipping and drops.

issues.title: Sizing & Fit
issues.blurb: Reported (~14%) — kids outgrow bikes fast, and a poor fit makes the bike awkward and harder to control.
